data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260508T015058-0647ab89/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260508T015058-0647ab89/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260508T015058-0647ab89/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260508T015058-0647ab89/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260508T015058-0647ab89/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-a41e6fbb">
    <title>agent: implement validated replacement safe rewrites</title>
    <description>
<context-digest>
<principles>Spec-first development · Executable specifications · Audit trail required · Context is king · Drift is debt</principles>
<concerns>go-std | security-owasp | testing</concerns>
<practices>Go changes live under cli/ · prefer focused unit tests with in-memory stubs for domain interfaces · run targeted go test plus lefthook · preserve audit evidence for automated mutations</practices>
<governing>FEAT-010 and ADR-023 now define safe rewrites as validated replacement rewrites: fit-for-purpose replacement is allowed when preservation is proven from durable anchors; original text belongs in readiness evidence, not duplicated into the bead prompt.</governing>
</context-digest>

PROBLEM
Safe rewrite implementation still enforces append-only description preservation even though the governing specs now require validated replacement rewrites. This keeps stale/noisy bead text in the implementation prompt and rejects useful replacements; it also fails the opposite case, where a one-line vague bead must expand into a standalone executable task.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_rewrite.go:118-120 rejects any description rewrite unless the normalized old description appears as a substring of the new description.
- cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:183-188 prompts the intake model to return rewrites but does not say replacement rewrites should optimize prompt fitness, validate against durable anchors, and preserve original text through evidence rather than prompt duplication.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:176-211 and docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-023-bead-lifecycle-quality-policy.md:136-166 now require validated replacement rewrites, prompt fitness, and audit evidence.
- cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_rewrite.go:60-80 records before/after hashes but does not preserve enough original/replacement evidence or preservation rationale for audit when the old body is replaced.

PROPOSED FIX
- Replace the substring-only description guard with a deterministic validated-replacement policy. The validator must preserve explicit commitments: acceptance criteria, non-scope, governing artifact references, dependencies, named files/tests, and still-valid root-cause evidence, or replace stale evidence with current durable file:line/section anchors.
- Allow both directions of prompt-fitness improvement: expanding underspecified beads with necessary durable context, and compressing noisy/stale beads while preserving explicit commitments.
- Keep rejection behavior for invented behavior, changed scope, deleted unresolved constraints, or missing preservation evidence.
- Update the intake prompt to ask for fit-for-purpose replacement rewrites, durable preservation evidence, and explicit ambiguity instead of append-only amendments.
- Extend the `intake-rewritten` event body so original/replacement audit evidence and preservation rationale are available without embedding the old body back into the new prompt. Use hashes plus bounded excerpts or attachment pointers consistent with existing event-size conventions.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement child decomposition changes.
- Do not relax acceptance criteria preservation.
- Do not allow product-semantic choices to be made by the rewrite validator.
- Do not rename legacy `PreClaimIntake*` structs in this bead.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-5cd555c0. Depends on ddx-5cd555c0 so the spec-evolution bead lands first.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. TestPreClaimIntakeRewrite_AllowsCompressionReplacement verifies a replacement description can remove stale/noisy old prose when explicit commitments and durable anchors are preserved.
2. TestPreClaimIntakeRewrite_AllowsExpansionReplacement verifies a vague one-line description can expand into a standalone executable bead when added content is grounded in durable context and does not invent product semantics.
3. TestPreClaimIntakeRewrite_RejectsDeletedCommitment verifies non-scope, governing artifact references, named files/tests, or unresolved constraints cannot be silently removed.
4. TestPreClaimIntakeRewrite_RecordsReplacementEvidence verifies `intake-rewritten` records changed fields, rationale, before/after hashes, and preservation evidence sufficient to audit the original text without duplicating it into the replacement prompt body.
5. TestPreClaimIntakePrompt_AsksForFitForPurposeValidatedReplacement verifies the prompt tells the model to optimize prompt fitness, avoid append-only noise, and classify ambiguous cases as needs-human.
6. Existing TestIntake_ActionableButRewritten_UpdatesAfterClaim and TestIntake_UnsafeRewriteBlocksForHuman remain green.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestPreClaimIntakeRewrite|TestIntake_.*Rewrite" -count=1 passes.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:agent, area:bead, kind:feature, reliability, bead-quality, spec:FEAT-010, spec:ADR-023, from:ddx-5cd555c0</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-5cd555c0" spec-id="FEAT-010" base-rev="5480546a547a822ed9fb41cb57bf9d8451aeb94c" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260508T015058-0647ab89"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-010" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md">Feature: Task Execution</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
All ACs satisfied and committed. Here's what was done:

**Changes committed in `feat(agent): replace append-only intake check with validated replacement policy [ddx-a41e6fbb]`** (the code was in the prior checkpoint):

1. **`cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_rewrite.go`** — Replaced the substring containment check (lines 118-120) with `validateDescriptionReplacement`, which extracts durable commitments (governing refs like FEAT-NNN/ADR-NNN, named test functions, file:line anchors, dep IDs, NON-SCOPE bullets) and requires each to be present in the replacement. Extended `preClaimIntakeRewriteEventBody` with `PreservationEvidence []string`. Updated `validateAndApplyPreClaimIntakeRewrite` signature to return preservation evidence.

2. **`cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go`** — Updated the prompt to ask for fit-for-purpose replacement rewrites, explain preservation rules, and classify ambiguous cases as `ambiguous_needs_human`.

3. **`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_intake_test.go`** — Updated `TestIntake_DescriptionPreservationFailureParksForHuman` and `TestIntake_UnsafeRewriteRemovedFromReadyExecution` to use descriptions with explicit commitments (NON-SCOPE sections) so they correctly test the new commitment-based rejection.

4. **`cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_rewrite_test.go`** (new) — Five tests covering AC1–AC5: compression allowed, expansion allowed, commitment deletion rejected, replacement evidence recorded, prompt asks for fit-for-purpose rewrites.
